You are auditing a code-review-readiness initiative against its authoritative plan, Plan V4 (read it in full: /home/polaris/wt/phase1/PLAN_V4_REFERENCE.md).
The ONE inviolable rule of the plan: no change may move the pipeline RACE score or faithfulness behavior; every change must be provably byte-identical/score-safe.
Two git worktrees hold the committed work:
- Phase 0 work: /home/polaris/wt/phase0 (branch chore/review-readiness-phase0, GitHub PR #1381)
- Phase 1 work: /home/polaris/wt/phase1 (branch chore/review-readiness-phase1, GitHub PR #1382)
Be EVIDENCE-BASED: run git log/ls/grep/cat, read tests and docs, and cite concrete SHAs/paths/counts. Do NOT assume something is done because a doc claims it — verify the artifact exists and matches the plan's acceptance wording. Mark PARTIAL when the artifact exists but does not fully meet the plan's stated acceptance. Report ONLY your assigned area.

YOUR AREA: Phase 1 (central settings). Start by: cd /home/polaris/wt/phase1 && git log --oneline -25 and ls the tree.
Phase 1 items: 1A govern+classify ALL ~923 keys (supported/internal/secret/deprecated/experimental) each with owner+precedence, gate=100% classified+owned; 1B FULL-BEHAVIOR characterization tests for every key across {unset,empty,valid,malformed} x {default/.env/process-env/CLI precedence} x {key-case} x {read-timing lazy vs import-snapshot} x {runtime type str/coerced/SecretStr}; 1C migrate one module at a time, each default=exact current value, env names preserved, SecretStr for secrets with call-sites unwrapped, acceptance harness after each module. CRITICAL: verify what was ACTUALLY done — settings.py, config_defaults.py, the 832-site AST migration (commit dd96ceb), characterization tests test_settings_models.py + test_config_registry.py. Assess: does 1B's matrix actually cover precedence/case/timing/malformed/SecretStr, or only byte-identical default resolution? Did 1C follow 'one module at a time' or a bulk codemod, and is that deviation safe? Are secrets migrated to SecretStr yet? Check git log on the branch.

Return every plan item in your area with a status and concrete evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Deep Cove Research — Code Review Readiness Plan (v4)
2	
3	**Goal:** make the code clean and professional enough to pass an independent Telus code review — **without ever changing the pipeline's RACE score or its faithfulness behavior.**
4	
5	**Date:** 2026-07-18 · **Status:** for owner approval (v4 — folds in three rounds of independent codex review + the exhaustive name-read worklist)
6	
7	**Version history:**
8	- v1 — first draft (research+audit workflow).
9	- v2 — codex round 1 (baseline first; "byte-identical" needs a real test; severity recalibrated; reorder).
10	- v3 — codex round 2 (baseline *manifest* + comparison protocol; config precedence/case/timing/type tests + govern-and-own keys before migrating; graph removal gated on compatibility matrix + usage evidence + repeated replays; missing axes → scheduled gated deliverables; renames gated on public-compatibility check).
11	- **v4 — codex round 3 + the name-read results:** (a) baseline manifest is **secret-safe** (digests, never raw secret values); (b) the **governing RACE/faithfulness measurement is never quarantined** — it is characterized statistically or execution stops; (c) **real owners/roles assigned**, no `TBD`; and (d) Phase 2 now carries the **concrete 210-name rename worklist** produced by reading every line of all 842 files.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Roles (concrete — no `TBD`)
16	
17	- **Approver / risk-acceptance:** the **project owner** (you). Signs off each phase; accepts residual risk; no phase starts without approval.
18	- **Executor:** the **engineering agent (Opus), under owner supervision.** Does the work, runs the acceptance harness, produces evidence.
19	- **Independent reviewer:** **codex (GPT-5.6)** — adversarial checks at each gate.
20	
21	Every workstream below names an **Owner (Approver) + Executor**.
22	
23	---
24	
25	## The one rule (never break this)
26	
27	The pipeline's **RACE score** and its **faithfulness behavior** must stay **exactly the same** through every change. No change may move a threshold, a model name, a temperature, or a verdict. If we cannot *prove* a change is safe, we do not ship it.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## The good news: the code is better than it feels
32	
33	- **96%** of functions type-annotated · **98.5%** module-docstring coverage · **1,082 tests** exist · **secrets clean** (no keys in git) · STORM/FS-Researcher are **clean-room rewrites from the papers, cited — not copied** (a naming choice, not a legal problem).
34	
35	→ The gap is **discipline a reviewer can see, plus a few unknowns we must turn into knowns.**
36	
37	---
38	
39	## The real problems (honest severity)
40	
41	1. **HIGH — Tests not enforced.** 1,082 tests never run in CI; no lint/type-check/`pyproject.toml`; 28 CI actions on moving tags.
42	2. **HIGH — Settings scattered.** 1,707 `os.getenv` calls, ~923 keys, 24 documented; model names/numbers hardcoded in 9+ files.
43	3. **HIGH — One real architecture mess:** the live 3-way `graph.py`/`graph_v2`/`graph_v3` fork chosen by env var — the single most dangerous change.
44	4. **MEDIUM — Unprofessional naming (tidiness):** confirmed by reading every line — **210 validated renames** (see Phase 2), family leaders: `honest` ×37, version-markers ×44, `junk` ×29, `beat-both` ×12, `lethal` ×7, internal codenames ×14, cryptic abbreviations ×55.
45	5. **MEDIUM — A checkpoint built but never turned on** → every restart re-runs 30–40 min generation.
46	6. **NARROW — Docs gaps only in contract/API files** (~530 docstrings).
47	
48	**Plus six unknowns a reviewer will probe** → the Scheduled Deliverables table.
49	
50	---
51	
52	## The plan — step by step
53	
54	Referee for "did the pipeline change?": the acceptance harness (`acceptance_outline_agent.py`), governed by the Phase-0 baseline manifest + comparison protocol. The graph fork additionally needs repeated per-selector replays.
55	
56	### Phase 0 — Build a real baseline, then only the truly-safe tidy-ups
57	**Owner (Approver): project owner · Executor: engineering (Opus).**
58	
59	**0A. The reproducible baseline.**
60	
61	*0A-1 — Record a SECRET-SAFE baseline manifest* (`baseline/manifest.json`): git commit SHA; `requirements.lock` hash; Python version + OS; the provider/model routing config; seeds; exact commands; input fixtures.
62	- **Env values:** record every env-var **name** and its **non-secret** value verbatim. For any **secret** (API keys, tokens, credentials): record **only a SHA-256 digest + a "present/absent" flag — never the raw value.** The manifest carries **no plaintext secret**, is written to a git-ignored location with documented access, and is safe to share with a reviewer.
63	
64	*0A-2 — Run in an ISOLATED sandbox* (running the pipeline is **not** read-only — it writes files and calls providers): dedicated scratch output dir; GitHub backup daemon **paused**; no writes to shared state; provider calls recorded or run via the existing offline/deterministic acceptance path. Record every output artifact **and its SHA-256 hash**.
65	
66	*0A-3 — Establish non-flakiness — but NEVER quarantine the governing measurement.* Run the test suite **and** the acceptance harness **N≥3 times**; record pass/fail, timing, variance.
67	- **Only NON-governing unit tests may be quarantined** if flaky (into a tracked list).
68	- **The governing RACE score and faithfulness verdicts are NEVER quarantined.** They are **characterized statistically** — mean and spread over N repeats, with a declared equivalence band. **If they cannot be characterized within a stable band, that is a BLOCKING condition** (stop and investigate the source of variance) — not a quarantine. We do not proceed on an un-pinned oracle.
69	
70	*0A-4 — Comparison protocol* (declared before any change): "same" = the governing RACE score within the declared equivalence band over N repeats **and** identical faithfulness verdict sets on fixed inputs **and** artifact-hash match for artifacts meant to be deterministic. Document what is a **regression** vs noise.
71	
72	*0A-5 — Characterize all 3 graph selectors* on fixed inputs as **replay fixtures** (the "before" for each of v1/v2/v3).
73	
74	**0B. Changes that touch NO importable runtime code** (ship immediately): SHA-pin all 28 CI actions; fix third-party attribution; add `pyproject.toml` + CI in **report-only mode**; tidy README/architecture.
75	
76	**Deferred out of Phase 0** (only *looked* zero-risk): deleting "dead" files and changing secret types — wait for the baseline **and** the Phase-2 public-compatibility clearance.
77	
78	---
79	
80	### Phase 1 — Central settings (governance first, then full-behavior migration)
81	**Owner (Approver): project owner · Executor: engineering (Opus).**
82	
83	**1A. Govern + own the keys BEFORE moving any.** Classify **all ~923 keys** (including non-`PG_*`) as supported/internal/secret/deprecated/experimental, each with an **owner** and documented **precedence**. Gate: **100% classified + owned** before a single key migrates.
84	
85	**1B. Full-behavior characterization tests.** For every key, lock today's behavior across `{unset, empty, valid, malformed}` × `{default / .env / process-env / CLI precedence}` × `{key-case}` × `{read timing: lazy vs import-snapshot}` × `{runtime type: str / coerced / SecretStr wrapper}`. These are the real "byte-identical" proof.
86	
87	**1C. Migrate one module at a time.** Each default = today's exact value; each env name preserved; `SecretStr` for keys (call-sites updated to unwrap). Acceptance harness after each module; any deviation from the 0A baseline → stop. Never change a value in the same commit that moves it. Highest-risk: `state.py` (114 reads) and the model resolver.
88	
89	---
90	
91	### Phase 2 — Public-compatibility gate, THEN the 210-name cleanup + checkpoint
92	**Owner (Approver): project owner · Executor: engineering (Opus).**
93	
94	**2A. Public-compatibility inventory — runs BEFORE any rename/delete.** Inventory dynamic imports (`importlib`, `__import__`, `getattr` on modules, entry-points/plugins), string-based references, saved-state references, and external consumers. A name is cleared for rename/delete **only** after this shows nothing depends on it. "Zero static importers" is **not** sufficient.
95	
96	**2B. Execute the validated rename worklist** (`NAME_RENAME_WORKLIST_validated.tsv` — 210 renames, each with a risk class, produced by reading every line of all 842 files and QA'd by codex). **Execution order by risk:**
97	1. **105 `SAFE` symbol renames** (pure code symbols) — one per commit, acceptance harness before/after.
98	2. **12 `TEXT-ONLY`** (docstring/log-tag jargon — `BEAT-BOTH`, `MoST`, `GEMINI-ARCH`) — safe text edits.
99	3. **45 `FILE-RENAME`** — only after 2A clears each; `git mv` + fix importers; includes consolidating the 11 near-duplicate `run_full_scale_v10..v30` scripts into **one parameterized launcher**.
100	4. **32 `NEEDS-ALIAS`** — names that are control-surface / persisted strings (`PG_LETHAL_SEED_K`, `PG_JUNK_SOURCE_SCREEN`, `LIVE_HONEST`, `bond_analysis` dict keys, manifest keys): rename the **symbol** but **keep the old string working via an alias** — never a naive rename (would move the score).
101	- **16 `DOMAIN-REVIEW`** rows are surfaced to the owner for a keep/rename call.
102	- **135 names were validated as KEEP** (standard vocab / real version taxonomy / cited method names) — documented so we can tell Telus "checked, correct."
103	- **The `graph_v2/v3` version markers are NOT in this phase** — codex confirmed they are the real routed fork → **Phase 3**.
104	
105	**2C. Turn on the generation checkpoint.** Save **pre-check data only** (drafts, outline), never a verdict; **gate the write behind the flag** so normal runs are byte-identical; reload path re-verifies from scratch.
106	
107	---
108	
109	### Phase 3 — Docs, flakiness-gated required CI, then the graph fork (LAST)
110	**Owner (Approver): project owner · Executor: engineering (Opus).**
111	
112	**3A. Docs** — fill ~530 docstrings (contract/API first) + ADRs. Pure text.
113	
114	**3B. Flakiness policy BEFORE CI becomes required** — run the suite N times over several days; fix/quarantine flaky *non-governing* tests; define the non-flakiness bar. CI flips report-only → **required only after the suite is provably stable**, not after one green run. Add dependency hash-pinning + license/secret scanning here.
115	
116	**3C. The graph fork — fully gated.** Nothing in `graph*.py` deleted until ALL pass: (1) per-selector compatibility matrix on the 0A-5 fixtures; (2) real usage inventory (grep prod/CI/scripts/docs for `PG_GRAPH_VERSION` + `v2`/`v3` use); (3) saved-state migration fixtures for `ResearchStateV2`; (4) repeated equivalent replays per selector; (5) explicit rollback + deprecation window. Its own reviewed change.
117	
118	---
119	
120	## Scheduled deliverables — the six reviewer axes (owned, gated)
121	
122	| # | Deliverable | Acceptance | Gates (blocks) | Owner (Approver) / Executor |
123	|---|---|---|---|---|
124	| S1 | Config governance (classify+own 923 keys) | 100% classified + owned + precedence | Phase 1 migration | owner / Opus |
125	| S2 | Public-compatibility inventory + supported-Python/install/state-migration/rollback doc | inventory complete; nothing depends on renamed names | Phase 2 rename/delete | owner / Opus |
126	| S3 | Test quality / flakiness policy | suite stable across N runs; flaky (non-governing) quarantined; bar defined | Phase 3 required-CI | owner / Opus |
127	| S4 | Security / privacy review | threat-model doc (API auth, crawler SSRF, prompt-injection, PII, log redaction, checkpoint retention); no unresolved critical findings | before external exposure | owner / Opus |
128	| S5 | Operational readiness | checklist: timeouts, retries, rate/cost limits, monitoring, runbooks, recovery | before external exposure | owner / Opus |
129	| S6 | Reproducible build | two independent builds hash-match; hash-pinned deps + locked env + documented build + SBOM | before "reproducible" is claimed to Telus | owner / Opus |
130	
131	---
132	
133	## The safety rules (every change)
134	
135	1. **Baseline first** — no runtime change before the 0A secret-safe manifest + comparison protocol + N-run baseline exist, with the governing measurement statistically characterized (or execution blocked).
136	2. **Config = full-behavior proof** (value, precedence, case, coercion, malformed, timing, runtime type). Keys classified + owned before migration.
137	3. **No behavior change** in the same commit as a rename or a settings move.
138	4. **Renames/deletes gated on the public-compatibility inventory (S2)** — rename symbols/filenames only; env-var / enum / persisted string literals get an **alias**, never a naive rename.
139	5. **Checkpoint:** pre-check data only, never a verdict; write gated off by default.
140	6. **Acceptance harness before/after** every code-touching change; the graph fork additionally requires the full 3C gate.
141	7. **CI report-only → required only after the flakiness bar (S3)** — never gate cold.
142	8. **Secret-safe evidence** — no plaintext secret in any manifest/artifact; digests + access controls only.
143	9. **Do not rename real domain terms** (SmartArt, deep_crawl, OpenAlex, PubMed, Semantic Scholar) or the 135 codex-validated KEEP names. Keep paper citations.
144	
145	---
146	
147	## What to show Telus first
148	
149	1. The `pyproject.toml` + CI PR next to the **96% typing** number — quality now enforced.
150	2. The strong fundamentals, honestly (98.5% module docs, real README, 1,082 tests, pinned deps, clean secrets).
151	3. The clean-room proof for STORM/FS-Researcher.
152	4. The name-cleanup evidence (every line read; 210 scoped renames + 135 validated-correct) + this plan + the baseline manifest — proof the rest is scoped, owned, gated, and ring-fenced from the score.
153	
154	---
155	
156	## Where we start
157	
158	**Phase 0-A: the secret-safe reproducible baseline** (manifest → isolated N-run → statistically-characterized oracle → comparison protocol → 3-selector fixtures). It cannot change the pipeline, and every later "we did not move the score" claim depends on it.
159	
160	**One-line summary for the boss:** *"The pipeline is sound and the fundamentals are real; what's missing is the enforcement, the tidiness, and a few proofs an outsider needs. We establish a secret-safe evidence baseline first, then pay down each item — including 210 already-mapped renames — behind owned, gated guarantees that the score and faithfulness never move."*
161

1	# Configuration governance (Phase 1A)
2	
3	The pipeline's configuration surface, inventoried from **every** `os.getenv` / `os.environ` read site
4	in the code (not just `.env`), classified, and with the migration approach. This is the "govern before
5	migrate" gate: nothing migrates into the typed settings layer until it appears here.
6	
7	## The surface (measured)
8	- **1,644** distinct config keys read across `src/` + `scripts/` (2,592 read-sites).
9	- **1,044 runtime keys** (`src/polaris_graph`) — the governance + migration target:
10	
11	| Category | Count | Meaning | Migration priority |
12	|---|---:|---|---|
13	| model-selection | 35 | model slug / provider / LLM role choice | **P1 — the single source of truth** |
14	| secret | 97 | `*_KEY` / `*_TOKEN` / credentials | P1 — `SecretStr`, never plaintext |
15	| internal-flag | 71 | `_ENABLED`/`_MODE`/`_GATE` booleans | P2 |
16	| tuning-threshold | 372 | `MAX_`/`_CAP`/`_TIMEOUT`/`THRESHOLD`/`_K` numerics | P2 |
17	| external/system | 35 | non-`PG_` standard env (HOME, PATH, provider base urls) | keep as-is / thin wrapper |
18	| internal-other | 434 | other `PG_*` control flags | P3 (long tail) |
19	
20	- **600** keys are `scripts/`-only (dev tooling) — out of the runtime migration scope.
21	- **~1,198** keys are **code-default-only** (a literal default in the `os.getenv` call, not set in `.env`)
22	  → the migration MUST preserve each literal default exactly.
23	
24	Machine-readable inventory: `baseline/config_keys_classified.json` (git-ignored; per key: read-sites,
25	files, in-env, category, secret flag).
26	
27	## The hard rule (byte-identical by construction)
28	Every migrated key keeps its **exact `PG_*` name** and its **exact current default literal**. A
29	characterization test (Phase 1B) locks today's resolution behaviour — value, precedence, case,
30	coercion, malformed input, read-timing, runtime type — before any module migrates. Never change a
31	value in the same commit that moves it.
32	
33	## Approach (given the 1,044-key scale)
34	A one-shot rewrite of 1,044 hardcoded reads is neither safe nor reviewable. The plan is:
35	1. **Establish the pattern + the model source of truth first** — build `src/polaris_graph/settings.py`
36	   (pydantic-settings, `env_prefix="PG_"`, `SecretStr` for secrets) and migrate the **35
37	   model-selection keys** so "one file controls every model," which was the headline requirement.
38	2. **Migrate module-by-module** thereafter (start `abstractive_writer.py`), each behind a
39	   characterization test + the acceptance harness, as a tracked, gated backlog.
40	3. **A CI grep** bans *new* `os.getenv(` outside `settings.py`, so the surface stops growing while the
41	   backlog drains.
42	
43	Ownership: until per-key owners are assigned, the **project owner** is accountable for the model +
44	secret keys (P1); the executor (engineering) drains P2/P3 behind tests. No key migrates without a
45	green characterization test.
46	
47	## Progress (2026-07-19)
48	- **Done — foundation:** `settings.py` (`ModelSettings` for the 12 model keys + `resolve()` for the
49	  871-key single-default registry `config_defaults.py`), locked by characterization tests
50	  (`test_settings_models.py`, `test_config_registry.py` — resolve==os.getenv over all 871 keys).
51	- **Done — bulk call-site migration:** **832 `os.getenv("PG_X", lit)` sites across 130 modules**
52	  migrated to `resolve("PG_X")`, byte-identical, via an **AST codemod** (edits only real
53	  `os.getenv` Call nodes; strings/comments/docstrings structurally untouched; whole-file guards for
54	  any local `os`/`resolve` rebind). Verified: all 130 modules import, collection stays at the 11
55	  pre-existing baseline errors, heaviest modules' test suites behave identically to HEAD. Codex-gated
56	  (3 adversarial rounds → APPROVE).
57	- **Remaining tail (tracked, not blocking):**
58	  - **114 secret-shaped sites** → a separate `SecretStr` pass (behavior-changing; needs its own design).
59	  - **46 computed/multiline-default keys** → need typed per-domain accessors (can't be a static
60	    registry string).
61	  - **20 conflicting-default keys** → see [`config_conflicts.md`](config_conflicts.md); each needs a
62	    product decision on the single correct default before it can be centralized. One
63	    (`PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD`, 0.65 vs 0.75) touches the frozen-faithfulness invariant.
64	  - **33 non-PG external-convention vars** (`OPENAI_*`, `HF_*`, …) — left as-is (not our namespace).

1	"""Central, typed configuration — the single source of truth (Phase 1).
2	
3	This module replaces scattered ``os.getenv`` reads with one typed settings object. Phase 1 migrates
4	it in slices; this first slice is **model selection** (which model each role uses), because
5	"one file controls every model" was the headline requirement.
6	
7	Byte-identical rule: every field's default is the EXACT current literal, and every field maps to its
8	existing ``PG_*`` env var (via ``env_prefix``), so behaviour is unchanged. The characterization test
9	``tests/test_settings_models.py`` proves each field resolves identically to the current
10	``os.getenv(KEY, default)`` — both unset (default) and set (override).
11	
12	Nothing imports this yet; call sites migrate module-by-module behind that test (see
13	``docs/review_readiness/config_governance.md``). The remaining model keys with multi-line/computed
14	defaults (``PG_CONTRADICTION_MODEL``, ``PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL``, ``PG_RERANKER_MODEL``,
15	``PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_RERANKER_MODEL``) are added when their owning module migrates.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from pydantic import Field
21	from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
22	
23	
24	class ModelSettings(BaseSettings):
25	    """Model-selection config.
26	
27	    Each field reads its EXACT ``PG_*`` env var **case-sensitively** (via an explicit
28	    ``validation_alias``), matching ``os.getenv("PG_...")`` byte-for-byte — an ``env_prefix`` +
29	    case-insensitive setup would (wrongly) also match differently-cased names.
30	    """
31	
32	    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(case_sensitive=True, extra="ignore")
33	
34	    judge_model: str = Field("qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b", validation_alias="PG_JUDGE_MODEL")
35	    sentinel_model: str = Field("minimax/minimax-m2", validation_alias="PG_SENTINEL_MODEL")
36	    sweep_deepener_model: str = Field("deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro", validation_alias="PG_SWEEP_DEEPENER_MODEL")
37	    nli_model: str = Field("flan-t5-large", validation_alias="PG_NLI_MODEL")
38	    faithlens_model: str = Field("ssz1111/FaithLens", validation_alias="PG_FAITHLENS_MODEL")
39	    embed_model: str = Field("Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B", validation_alias="PG_EMBED_MODEL")
40	    embedder_model: str = Field("", validation_alias="PG_EMBEDDER_MODEL")
41	    planning_gate_model: str = Field("", validation_alias="PG_PLANNING_GATE_MODEL")
42	    policy_model: str = Field("", validation_alias="PG_POLICY_MODEL")
43	    outliner_agent_model: str = Field("z-ai/glm-5.2", validation_alias="PG_OUTLINER_AGENT_MODEL")
44	    outliner_code_model: str = Field("deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro", validation_alias="PG_OUTLINER_CODE_MODEL")
45	    evaluator_model: str | None = Field(None, validation_alias="PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL")
46	
47	
48	# Authoritative registry: env var -> (settings field, EXACT current default). The characterization
49	# test iterates this to prove byte-identical parity with today's os.getenv resolution.
50	MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS: dict[str, tuple[str, str | None]] = {
51	    "PG_JUDGE_MODEL": ("judge_model", "qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"),
52	    "PG_SENTINEL_MODEL": ("sentinel_model", "minimax/minimax-m2"),
53	    "PG_SWEEP_DEEPENER_MODEL": ("sweep_deepener_model", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"),
54	    "PG_NLI_MODEL": ("nli_model", "flan-t5-large"),
55	    "PG_FAITHLENS_MODEL": ("faithlens_model", "ssz1111/FaithLens"),
56	    "PG_EMBED_MODEL": ("embed_model", "Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B"),
57	    "PG_EMBEDDER_MODEL": ("embedder_model", ""),
58	    "PG_PLANNING_GATE_MODEL": ("planning_gate_model", ""),
59	    "PG_POLICY_MODEL": ("policy_model", ""),
60	    "PG_OUTLINER_AGENT_MODEL": ("outliner_agent_model", "z-ai/glm-5.2"),
61	    "PG_OUTLINER_CODE_MODEL": ("outliner_code_model", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro"),
62	    "PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL": ("evaluator_model", None),
63	}
64	
65	
66	def get_model_settings() -> ModelSettings:
67	    """Return a FRESH ``ModelSettings`` reading the CURRENT environment.
68	
69	    **Byte-identical migration contract.** ``os.getenv`` reads the current environment on *every*
70	    call; a ``ModelSettings`` instance instead *snapshots* the environment at construction. So a
71	    migrated call site must replace ``os.getenv("PG_X", default)`` with ``get_model_settings().x``
72	    (a fresh read), **never** a cached module-level instance — otherwise it would go stale relative
73	    to a runtime env change. Two model keys are set at process startup by scripts
74	    (``PG_NLI_MODEL`` via ``setdefault``, ``PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL`` from a CLI arg) and none are mutated
75	    mid-run, so a fresh read at the call site is exactly equivalent to the original ``os.getenv``.
76	    Construction is cheap (12 fields); the freshness guarantee is what preserves behaviour.
77	    """
78	    return ModelSettings()
79	
80	
81	# ── Long-tail config registry ────────────────────────────────────────────────
82	# For the ~871 non-model keys with a clean single-line default, a central registry provides a
83	# byte-identical alternative to scattered os.getenv. Typed accessors (like ModelSettings) are layered
84	# on top per-domain over time; resolve() is the mechanical, provably-equivalent first step.
85	from src.polaris_graph.config_defaults import CONFIG_DEFAULTS  # noqa: E402
86	
87	
88	def resolve(key: str) -> str | None:
89	    """Return ``os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key])`` — byte-identical to the original call site.
90	
91	    A migrated call site replaces ``os.getenv("PG_X", "<lit>")`` with ``resolve("PG_X")`` where the
92	    registry already holds ``"<lit>"``. Reads the CURRENT environment on every call (like os.getenv).
93	    """
94	    import os
95	
96	    if key not in CONFIG_DEFAULTS:
97	        raise KeyError(f"{key!r} is not a registered config key (multi-line/computed/conflicting — migrate manually)")
98	    return os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key])
99

4	  * UNSET  -> the field equals ``os.getenv(KEY, default)`` (the exact current default),

11	import os

21	        assert getattr(s, field) == os.getenv(key, default), f"unset resolution differs for {key}"

27	    s = ModelSettings()
28	    for key, (field, _default) in MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS.items():
29	        assert getattr(s, field) == os.getenv(key), f"override not honoured for {key}"
30	
31

33	    # os.getenv returns "" for an explicitly-empty var; ModelSettings must not silently coerce it.
34	    for key in MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS:
35	        monkeypatch.setenv(key, "")
36	    s = ModelSettings()
37	    for key, (field, _default) in MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS.items():
38	        assert getattr(s, field) == os.getenv(key), f"empty-string resolution differs for {key}"
39	
40	
41	def test_registry_matches_fields():
42	    # every registry entry maps to a real field, and the declared default equals the field default
43	    s = ModelSettings()  # env may be set in this process; only assert the field exists + is str|None
44	    for key, (field, default) in MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS.items():

51	    from src.polaris_graph.settings import get_model_settings
52	    monkeypatch.delenv("PG_JUDGE_MODEL", raising=False)

# Config finding: env vars with CONFLICTING default fallbacks

**Status:** discovered during Phase 1C config migration (2026-07-19). Not yet resolved — each needs a product decision on the single correct default.

These PG_ environment variables are read via `os.getenv(KEY, default)` at multiple call sites, but with **different** hardcoded fallback defaults. When the env var is unset, behavior therefore depends on *which code path* reads it first — a latent inconsistency. They were deliberately EXCLUDED from the central `resolve()` registry (which holds one default per key) because there is no single byte-identical value to adopt.

Each must be resolved by choosing the intended default, setting it in `.env` / the registry, and collapsing all call sites to it.

| Key | Conflicting defaults seen | Note |
|-----|---------------------------|------|
| `PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS` | '12', '2' |  |
| `PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_TIMEOUT` | '180', '240.0' |  |
| `PG_ANALYSIS_PIPELINE` | '', '8phase' |  |
| `PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_TIMEOUT` | '300', '600' |  |
| `PG_CONTENT_PER_SOURCE` | '10000', '25000' |  |
| `PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD` | '0.65', '0.75' | FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL: the frozen-faithfulness gate runs at two strictnesses (0.65 vs 0.75) depending on path. |
| `PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY` | '10', '5' |  |
| `PG_FETCH_TIMEOUT` | '20', '30' |  |
| `PG_GENERATOR_MODEL` | '', <none> |  |
| `PG_MAX_CITATION_FREQUENCY` | '5', '8' |  |
| `PG_MAX_CROSS_SOURCE_PAIRS` | '200', '50' |  |
| `PG_MAX_TOTAL_ACADEMIC` | '100', '500' |  |
| `PG_MAX_WORDS_PER_SECTION` | '1500', '2000' |  |
| `PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION` | '3', '5', '8' | Three distinct defaults (3/5/8) — evidence floor varies by path. |
| `PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_UTILIZATION` | '0.30', '0.40' | Utilization gate 0.30 vs 0.40. |
| `PG_MIN_QUOTE_WORDS` | '15', '5' |  |
| `PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS` | '0', '10000' | 0 vs 10000 — one path effectively disables the floor. |
| `PG_REACT_INTERPRET_TIMEOUT` | '180', '240' |  |
| `PG_REDUNDANCY_JACCARD_THRESHOLD` | '0.45', '0.65' | Dedup threshold 0.45 vs 0.65. |
| `PG_TARGET_TOTAL_WORDS` | '12000', '8000' | Report length target differs 8000 vs 12000. |

**Recommendation:** treat the faithfulness/evidence-gate keys (top of table) as priority — a divergent faithfulness threshold can silently weaken the frozen-faithfulness guarantee on one path.

I now have all the evidence. Let me compile the findings.

Key facts established:
- **1A governance**: classified JSON has 1,044 runtime keys, each with category/secret flag — but **0 entries have owner or precedence fields**. Governance doc assigns only a blanket owner ("project owner accountable for P1"), not per-key owners+precedence as the gate demands. - **1B characterization tests**: only cover {unset, empty, override} for default resolution. The plan's full matrix (precedence CLI/.env/process-env, key-case, read-timing lazy vs import-snapshot, malformed, SecretStr runtime type) is **NOT** covered — only 2 of the matrix's many axes (partial: unset/empty/valid default + one case-sensitivity check baked into the alias design).
- **1C migration**: dd96ceb is a **single bulk AST codemod** (832 sites, 130 files, one commit by polaris-bot), NOT one-module-at-a-time. The first 3 commits (6d8a733, f746662) did do module-by-module for 8 model keys, but the tail was bulk. The bulk deviation is byte-identical-by-construction (resolve returns os.getenv(k, default)), so it's score-safe, but **no acceptance harness was run per module** as 1C requires — verified byte-identity via import + test collection instead.
- **SecretStr**: **NOT done at all.** Zero SecretStr in source. 6 real API-key secrets sit in the plaintext `resolve()` registry as `str` (empty defaults, so no plaintext value leaks, but not typed). settings.py docstring says SecretStr for secrets but ModelSettings has none and the 114 secret sites are explicitly deferred.
- **512 os.getenv reads across 133 files remain** unmigrated. CI grep guard to ban new os.getenv is claimed in the doc but not implemented.
- **20 conflicting defaults** discovered including a FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL one (`PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD` 0.65 vs 0.75) — documented, not resolved.

items.gap: The matrix is largely NOT covered. Actually tested: unset + empty + valid-override default resolution (2-3 of the {value} axis) for the 871-key registry, plus lazy-vs-snapshot timing and case-sensitivity for the 12 model keys only. NOT tested for any key: malformed input; the .env / process-env / CLI precedence ordering axis (no .env or CLI layering is exercised at all — resolve() is pure os.getenv so precedence is untested by construction); key-case axis for the 871-key tail; read-timing for the 871 tail; runtime-type coercion (the int()/float()/=='1' wrappers live at call sites and are NOT characterized — a malformed 'abc' feeding int() would raise, uncharacterized); SecretStr runtime type (does not exist). So 1B proves byte-identical DEFAULT resolution (its stated aim in the doc) but not the full-behavior matrix the plan wording requires. grep for precedence|malformed|secretstr|import-snapshot|case-sensit test names in tests/ returns no dedicated config coverage.

items.title: Migrate ONE MODULE AT A TIME, acceptance harness after each module
items.evidence: First slices were per-module: 6d8a733 (nli_verifier, 1 file, 'first module'), f746662 (4 more modules, model keys). Then 166639a added config_defaults.py (871 keys) + resolve(). Then dd96ceb is a SINGLE commit migrating 832 os.getenv sites across 130 modules in one shot (author polaris-bot), described as 'AST-based codemod', +962/-832 lines. So the tail was a BULK codemod, not one-module-at-a-time.
items.gap: 1C says 'Migrate one module at a time... Acceptance harness after each module; any deviation from the 0A baseline -> stop.' The 832-site tail deviates: it is a bulk codemod across 130 modules in one commit. Verification was import-cleanliness (130/130), pytest --collect-only (11 pre-existing errors, no new), and characterization tests — NOT the acceptance_outline_agent.py harness. git log 6d8a733..c5448ec shows NO acceptance/harness/RACE mention in any Phase 1 migration commit body. The deviation is SCORE-SAFE by construction (resolve(k) == os.getenv(k, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[k]); coercion wrappers int()/float()/=='1' remain unchanged at call sites; registry default == exact call-site literal, test-proven over all 871 keys), and env names are preserved verbatim. But the plan's per-module acceptance-harness cadence was skipped in favor of static/import/collection equivalence proof.

items.title: SecretStr for secret keys, call-sites unwrapped
items.evidence: grep -rc SecretStr src/polaris_graph/ returns zero matches anywhere in the source tree. settings.py ModelSettings has no SecretStr fields. 6 real API-key secrets (EXA_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY, PG_OPENALEX_API_KEY, SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY, SERPER_API_KEY, ZYTE_API_KEY) sit in the plaintext config_defaults.py CONFIG_DEFAULTS as plain str (empty-string defaults). config_governance.md explicitly DEFERS this: '114 secret-shaped sites -> a separate SecretStr pass (behavior-changing; needs its own design).'
items.gap: Secrets are NOT migrated to SecretStr — the requirement is entirely deferred. No plaintext secret VALUE leaks (all secret defaults are ''), so no security regression was introduced, and deferral is arguably correct since SecretStr wrapping/unwrapping is behavior-changing (violates the byte-identical-in-one-commit rule). But as a plan item it is unstarted: no SecretStr type, no design doc for the unwrap pass, and 114 secret sites still read as plain os.getenv.

items.title: Byte-identical value preserved (no value changed while moving it); conflicting defaults handled safely
items.evidence: config_conflicts.md (commit c5448ec) documents 20 PG_ keys read with DIFFERENT hardcoded defaults at different call sites, deliberately EXCLUDED from the resolve() registry (no single byte-identical value). Includes PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD (0.65 vs 0.75) flagged FAITHFULNESS-CRITICAL, PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION (3/5/8), PG_MIN_TOTAL_WORDS (0 vs 10000). The migrated keys are byte-identical by construction and correctly leave these conflicts untouched.
items.gap: The 20 conflicting-default keys (incl. a faithfulness-gate threshold that runs at two strictnesses depending on code path) are DOCUMENTED but UNRESOLVED — 'each needs a product decision'. This is a latent pre-existing inconsistency Phase 1 surfaced but did not fix; correctly out of the byte-identical migration, but it means the frozen-faithfulness invariant already has a path-dependent divergence (0.65 vs 0.75) that remains live. Also: 512 os.getenv reads across 133 src files remain unmigrated (secret/computed/conflicting/non-PG tail), and the doc's claimed 'CI grep bans new os.getenv outside settings.py' guard is NOT implemented (no such check in .github/ or .pre-commit-config.yaml).

input.area_summary: Phase 1 (central settings) landed a real, score-safe foundation but is materially short of Plan V4's stated acceptance on three of its own gates. WHAT'S SOLID: a typed settings.py with the 12 model-selection keys as the single source of truth (byte-identical, case-sensitive aliases), an auto-generated 871-key CONFIG_DEFAULTS registry with resolve(), and an 832-site AST codemod (dd96ceb) that routes non-secret PG_ reads through resolve() — byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (resolve(k)==os.getenv(k,default), coercion wrappers untouched at call sites, env names preserved, registry default==exact literal, test-proven over all 871 keys). This does not move the RACE score. WHERE IT FALLS SHORT OF THE PLAN: (1A) all 1,044 runtime keys are classified but 0 carry per-key owner/precedence — the gate demands '100% classified + OWNED with documented precedence before a single key migrates,' and migration proceeded on a blanket-owner note instead. (1B) the 'full-behavior' characterization is really byte-identical DEFAULT-resolution only: unset/empty/override for the 871 tail plus timing+case for the 12 model keys — the plan's precedence (.env/process-env/CLI), malformed-input, key-case-for-tail, coercion-type, and SecretStr axes are untested. (1C) the tail was a BULK codemod across 130 modules in one commit, not one-module-at-a-time, and the per-module acceptance_outline_agent.py harness was never run (equivalence proven via import + collection + characterization instead) — a safe-but-off-process deviation. SecretStr is entirely MISSING (zero occurrences in src; 6 API-key secrets sit as plain str in the registry; the whole secret pass is deferred). Phase 1 also surfaced but did NOT resolve 20 conflicting-default keys, one of them (PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD 0.65 vs 0.75) a live faithfulness-gate divergence, and 512 os.getenv reads across 133 files remain unmigrated with the promised CI grep-guard unimplemented. Net: the committed changes appear byte-identical/score-safe, but the plan items are DONE-by-construction rather than DONE-by-the-acceptance-wording, and two required deliverables (per-key ownership+precedence, SecretStr) are absent.
